purpose: Day-8 THIRD external axis — clinical-trial OUTCOME DIRECTION (drug SUCCESS vs FAILURE in autoimmune indications) vs the committed binary GO/WITHHELD gate. Scores the REAL committed gate (commit_gate.evaluate reproduced on the 98-gene universe = 49 GO/49 WITHHELD), NOT a surrogate.
honesty_contract: Labels+evidence hash-frozen BEFORE scoring (prereg 78448a46, labels 4910d817). No tuning. Real gate only. Negatives/nulls reported plainly incl. gate's own false negatives.

real_gate_reproduction.rule: GO iff ot_t1d>=0.20 AND eqtl_nsig>0 (trust non-binding, leakage-clean by construction, direction not-inconsistent at scale)
real_gate_reproduction.eqtl_source: EBI eQTL Catalogue API v2, 11 T-cell datasets (QTD000031/036/464/469/479/484/600/611/612/613/614), nlog10p>=5.0
real_gate_reproduction.universe_split: 49 GO / 49 WITHHELD over 98 genes — EXACT match to the prior committed run (faithful reproduction, not a proxy)

baseline_comparison.existence_RETRACTED_baseline: DEGENERATE on the direction axis: all scored genes have an autoimmune drug program by construction, so has_any_autoimmune_drug=1 for all 8/10 => 0 discrimination (MCC undefined/0). This is exactly why 'drug existence' is a weak axis and 'outcome direction' is the stronger test.
baseline_comparison.ot_t1d_assoc: continuous OT-T1D score does NOT order SUCCESS>FAILURE: IL10 (FAILURE) has ot_t1d=0.533, higher than 5 of 7 SUCCESS genes => association-alone is anti-correlated with outcome here.
baseline_comparison.eqtl_nsig: eqtl_nsig is the gate's binding variable; all 3 FAILURES have eqtl_nsig=0 while 5/7 SUCCESS have eqtl_nsig>0 => the gate's own signal carries the direction.

CD3E.evidence: Teplizumab (anti-CD3E) FDA-approved 2022 to delay T1D onset; multiple approved anti-CD3 (muromonab, blinatumomab). maxStage=APPROVAL.

CD52.evidence: Alemtuzumab (anti-CD52) approved for relapsing multiple sclerosis. maxStage=APPROVAL.

CD6.evidence: Itolizumab (anti-CD6) approved (India, plaque psoriasis) / Phase III SLE & aGVHD. maxStage=PHASE_3.

FKBP1A.evidence: Tacrolimus/sirolimus/pimecrolimus (FKBP1A binders) approved immunosuppressants (transplant, atopic dermatitis, psoriasis). maxStage=APPROVAL.

IL2RB.evidence: Basiliximab/daclizumab (anti-IL2R) approved (transplant rejection; daclizumab was approved for MS). maxStage=APPROVAL.

PPIA.evidence: Cyclosporine (cyclophilin/PPIA binder) approved for RA, psoriasis, atopic dermatitis. maxStage=APPROVAL.

TNF.evidence: Adalimumab/etanercept/infliximab (anti-TNF) approved across RA, IBD, psoriasis — cornerstone autoimmune therapy. maxStage=APPROVAL.

IL10.evidence: Recombinant human IL-10 (ilodecakin/tenovil) failed in large placebo-controlled RCTs in Crohn's disease and RA (no efficacy; dose-dependent anemia, PMID 12165551 n=329); no approved autoimmune drug for the target.

IL7R.evidence: Anti-IL7R programs: RN168/PF T1D stopped at Phase 1 (NCT02038764); OSE-127/lusvertikimab UC Phase 2 completed (later mixed/positive). No approved autoimmune drug for the target; net program non-advancement, but NOT an unambiguous efficacy failure.

GATA3.evidence: SB010 (GATA3 DNAzyme) showed a POSITIVE Phase 2a asthma signal (Krug NEJM 2015, PMID 25981191); SB012 UC did not advance. No approved drug; borderline — early positive signal means this is NOT a clean failure.

honest_verdict: DIRECTION IS RIGHT-SIGNED BUT UNDERPOWERED. Among the 98 gate-scored genes, only 7 carry a genuine autoimmune immune-mechanism drug program and ALL 7 SUCCEEDED (approved/Phase-III); clean documented target-level efficacy FAILURES are near-absent (recombinant IL-10 in Crohn's/RA is the one unambiguous case, PMID 12165551). On the primary SUCCESS(7)-vs-FAILURE-strict(1) set the gate is perfectly specific (WITHHELD the 1 failure, IL10) and 5/7 sensitive (WITHHELD two successes, IL2RB & TNF, both eqtl_nsig=0): confusion [TP5,FP0,FN2,TN1], MCC +0.49, bal-acc 0.86 — but Fisher one-sided p=0.375, NOT significant at n=8. Adding the two borderline failures (IL7R, GATA3, both WITHHELD) keeps specificity 1.0, lifts MCC to +0.66, and moves Fisher to p=0.083 — a consistent but still non-significant trend. The RETRACTED drug-existence baseline is DEGENERATE on this axis (every scored gene has a drug, so it cannot separate success from failure), and OT-association-alone is anti-correlated (IL10 outranks 5/7 successes). So the gate's direction is real and the right sign — it never GO'd a failure — but the autoimmune-outcome axis cannot reach significance because the FAILURE class is essentially empty (n=1 clean). Reported as UNDERPOWERED, not a win. This does not add an independent external win beyond the surviving mouse-KO axis; it is a corroborating-direction null.
